{{Quote|Why work up a sweat when I got a mountain full o' followers t'do my punchin' for me?|Gideon Longspar|<ref name="marvel66" />}}
'''Gideon Longspar''' was a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] and bandit who, in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], terrorized the [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmers]] of [[Beheboth]] by raiding their [[Moisture farm/Legends|farms]] for every drop of [[Water/Legends|water]] they had and re-selling it on the [[Black market/Legends|black market]]. Longspar and his [[Brigand|gang]] accomplished this after discovering how to manipulate and control the [[Tirrith]], an empathic gaseous culture of hive-minded [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] particles that became key to his scheme. However, local farmer [[Darial Anglethorn]] and her friend [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] joined forces with the vengeful Tirrith to defeat Longspar and destroy his operation.

==Biography==
{{Quote|The kid's come to rescue ya! He's a regular ''hero!'' Only I'm afraid he's gonna be disappointed! 'Cause ''I'' got plans for ya m'self, li'l lady, ya been a thorn in my side fer too long—an' yer about t'be ''plucked!''|Gideon Longspar, to Darial Anglethorn|<ref name="marvel66" />}}
[[File:Whadak.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Luke Skywalker knocks Gideon Longspar out cold.]]
Formerly a first-contact specialist<ref name="gamer7">{{GamerCite|7|Alien Anthology Addendum|''Alien Anthology'' Addendum}}</ref> who encountered [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="marvel66" /> Gideon Longspar had failed in his previous profession by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="gamer7">{{GamerCite|7|Alien Anthology Addendum|''Alien Anthology'' Addendum}}</ref> when he had come to lead a gang of [[brigand]]s on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Beheboth]]. Longspar encountered a unique creature called the [[Tirrith]], an empathic gaseous culture of hive-minded [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] particles—after realizing the immense power that large groups of Tirrith had when joined together, Longspar captured a group of Tirrith and held them hostage to ensure the rest of the collective's cooperation in a lucrative new scheme. Beheboth was a desert world that had by that time not experienced [[Water/Legends|rain]] in many [[Standard year/Legends|years]], meaning water was extremely valuable—Longspar began a series of devastating raids on local [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farms]], using the Tirrith to put any defenders to sleep before taking every bit of water and reselling it for a fortune on the [[Black market/Legends|black market]].<ref name="marvel66" />

Operating out of a remote mountain base, Longspar and his gang successfully robbed a number of farms in the vicinity of the town of [[Prosperity (Beheboth)|Garrotine]], driving many of the locals to desperation. Longspar eventually took a crack at the largest moisture farm on the planet, in doing so kidnapping owner [[Darial Anglethorn]], who had taken a leading role in organizing farmers to defend their homes. In response, Anglethorn's friend [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] officer stranded on Beheboth due to a damaged [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]—stormed Longspar's stronghold himself, only to be captured and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] along with Anglethorn. A vengeful Tirrith helped free Longspar's prisoners, and when Longspar attempted to waylay them with Skywalker's confiscated [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], Skywalker instead maneuvered Longspar into accidentally destroying the Tirrith's [[Tirrith restraining globe|restraining globe]] and freeing the remainder of the culture. Skywalker then knocked Longspar out cold with a left hook, and Anglethorn followed by destroying Longspar's water tanks, wiping out his operation and ending the threat of his brigands.<ref name="marvel66" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Boy, I suggest ya watch yer tongue... while ya've still ''got'' one!|Gideon Longspar, to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="marvel66" />}}
A ruthless bandit without empathy for others, Gideon Longspar was both boastful of his achievements and personally lazy—Longspar did not believe in doing work that someone else could do for him instead. A cruel man willing to consign many to die in order to make a profit, Longspar was personally confident in his own abilities, beleiving that he could effectively use a lightsaber despite not having seen such a weapon in decades.<ref name="marvel66" />

==Behind the scenes==
Gideon Longspar appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 66|''Star Wars'' (1977) 66]], written by [[David Michelinie]], illustrated by [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]] and released in [[1982]].<ref name="marvel66" />
